summary: Read-only R9 review completed. Public-data and sync policy coverage is mostly sufficient as a contract, but release/completion remains conditional on real safety verifier execution, dirty data repo cleanup, and policy model reconciliation.
decisions: Repos are currently public by GitHub API, so public-safe data rules apply.
Raw transcript controls are strong: local-only raw archives, redaction/metadata retention, and blocked raw paths.
Candidate/review queue handling is still operationally risky because the real data repo has a large tracked/untracked queue backlog.
Data hooks are configured, but current dirty state is not push-clean.
Node is missing on PATH, so npm safety checks were not rerun.
next_steps: Run safety:public-data:check with the installed Node runtime.
Clean or explicitly classify candidate/review/operational backlog before push.
Reconcile git_sync and public-data verifier classifications, especially .dino/gates.
